title: ISE Stealth-Mode Posture and Compliance Pre-Auth Validation

compliance.assurance_rationale: A.8.7 malware protection — stealth posture is one such control.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Misconfigure stealth posture to require an unreachable file. Many endpoints fail silently. UC-17.1.63 fires.

dataSources: ISE syslog `index=nac sourcetype=cisco:ise:syslog` *Posture* category. Stealth-mode codes: `15060` (stealth posture initiated), `15061` (stealth posture passed), `15062` (stealth posture failed). Differentiates from interactive posture (15041-15046).
spl:
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (15060, 15061, 15062)
| stats count by MessageCode, posture_policy
| eval action = case(MessageCode=="15061", "pass", MessageCode=="15062", "fail")
| eventstats sum(eval(if(action=="fail",count,0))) as fails sum(count) as total by posture_policy
| eval fail_rate = round((fails/total)*100, 1)
| where fail_rate > 20
description: Tracks stealth-mode posture (where the user isn't prompted) and detects high failure rates that indicate misconfigured stealth policies impacting users without their knowledge.
value: Stealth posture is invisible to users. A failed stealth check might silently restrict access. Monitoring failure rate ensures the silent enforcement isn't excluding legitimate users.
knownFalsePositives: (a) Initial rollout — high fail rate before agent push completes.
mitreAttack: N/A (policy hygiene — measures stealth-mode posture coverage, no adversary technique)
grandmaExplanation: Some compliance checks happen invisibly while you log in. We watch how often these silent checks fail — too high a failure rate means real users are being silently held back.
